chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ وَ الْعُمْرَةِ وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Merits of the Hajj and the Umra, and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/130)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/130)
text: قَالَ رَجُلٌ لِعَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) تَرَكْتَ الْجِهَادَ وَ خُشُونَتَهُ وَ لَزِمْتَ الْحَجَّ وَ لِينَهُ قَالَ وَ كَانَ مُتَّكِئاً فَجَلَسَ وَ قَالَ وَيْحَكَ أَ مَا بَلَغَكَ مَا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ إِنَّهُ لَمَّا وَقَفَ بِعَرَفَةَ وَ هَمَّتِ الشَّمْسُ أَنْ تَغِيبَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَا بِلَالُ قُلْ لِلنَّاسِ فَلْيُنْصِتُوا فَلَمَّا نَصَتُوا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ تَطَوَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ فَغَفَرَ لِمُحْسِنِكُمْ وَ شَفَّعَ مُحْسِنَكُمْ فِي مُسِيئِكُمْ فَأَفِيضُوا مَغْفُوراً لَكُمْ
قَالَ وَ زَادَ غَيْرُ الثُّمَالِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ إِلَّا أَهْلَ التَّبِعَاتِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَدْلٌ يَأْخُذُ لِلضَّعِيفِ مِنَ الْقَوِيِّ

فَلَمَّا كَانَتْ لَيْلَةُ جَمْعٍ لَمْ يَزَلْ يُنَاجِي رَبَّهُ وَ يَسْأَلُهُ لِأَهْلِ التَّبِعَاتِ فَلَمَّا وَقَفَ بِجَمْعٍ قَالَ لِبِلَالٍ قُلْ لِلنَّاسِ فَلْيُنْصِتُوا فَلَمَّا نَصَتُوا قَالَ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ تَطَوَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ فَغَفَرَ لِمُحْسِنِكُمْ وَ شَفَّعَ مُحْسِنَكُمْ فِي مُسِيئِكُمْ فَأَفِيضُوا مَغْفُوراً لَكُمْ وَ ضَمِنَ لِأَهْلِ التَّبِعَاتِ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ الرِّضَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Ayoub, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly who said,

‘A man said to Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, ‘You<sup>asws</sup> neglected the Jihad and necessitated the Hajj and its softness?’ He said, ‘And he<sup>asws</sup> was reclining, so he<sup>asws</sup> sat up and said: ‘Woe be unto you! Has it not reached you what Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said in the Farewell Hajj? It was when he<sup>saww</sup> had paused at Arafat and the sun had inclined to set, Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘O Bilal! Say to the people, so let them be silent’. So when they were silent, Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Your Lord<sup>azwj</sup> has Extended (Favours) upon you in this day, so He<sup>azwj</sup> Forgave your good-doers, and Interceded for your good-doers regarding your evil-doers. Therefore depart (with your sins having been) Forgiven for you’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘And (narrators) other than Al-Sumaly have increased that, ‘He<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Except for the people with the liabilities, for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is Just. He<sup>azwj</sup> Takes for the weak ones, from the strong ones’.

So when it was the night of Friday, he<sup>saww</sup> did not cease to whisper to his<sup>saww</sup> Lord<sup>azwj</sup>, and he<sup>saww</sup> asked Him<sup>azwj</sup> for the people with the liabilities. So when he<sup>saww</sup> paused at Jam’a, said to Bilal: ‘Say to the people, so let them be silent!’ So when they were silent, he<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Your Lord<sup>azwj</sup> has Extended (Favours) upon you in this day, so He<sup>azwj</sup> Forgave your good-doers, and Interceded for your good-doers regarding your evil-doers. Therefore depart (with your sins having been) Forgiven for you, and He<sup>azwj</sup> has Taken the Responsibility for the people with the liabilities of the pleasure (of their creditors) from His<sup>azwj</sup> Presence’.
en.sarwar: 24. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Ayyub from abu Hamzah al-Thumafiy who has said the following: “A man once said to Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘You have given up Jihad because it is harsh, but you have attached yourself to Hajj because it is comfortable.’ He (the Imam) who was leaning sat up straight and said, ‘Fie upon you. Have you not heard what the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, said during his farewell pilgrimage during his stay in ‘Arafat, and it was about sunset. The Messenger of Allah said, ‘O Bilal, ask people to remain silent.’ When people became silent the Messenger of Allah said, ‘Your Lord has granted you favors on this day. He has granted forgiveness to the individuals of good deeds, and has made the ones of good deed to intercede for the ones of evil deeds. You can leave now with your sins forgiven.’ Narrators other than abu Hamzah al-Thumaliy have mentioned that the Imam additionally said, ‘Except those who are of individuals of al-Tabi‘at (acts that require investigation).’ Allah is just. He brings to justice the stronger ones who wrong the weaker ones. On the night of al-Jam‘ he continued quietly speaking to his Lord and asked Him for forgiveness for the individuals of al-Tabi‘at. When he stayed in Jam‘ he said to Bilal. ‘You should ask people to remain silent.’ When people became silent he (the Messenger of Allah) said, ‘Your Lord has granted you favors on this day. He has forgiven the individuals of good deeds among you. He has made the individuals of good deeds among you to intercede on behalf of the individuals of evil deeds. You now may leave with your sins forgiven and He has made an undertaking in favor of the individuals of al- Tabi‘at (people liable for the rights of others) to be happy with them.’”
